author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter VIII. 12.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What we have just heard and attentively received, as the holy Gospel was being read, I doubt not that all of us have also endeavored to understand, and that each of us according to his measure apprehended what he could of so great a matter as that which has been read; and while the bread of the word is laid out, no one can complain that he has tasted nothing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But again I doubt not that there is scarcely any who has understood the whole.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nevertheless, even should there be any who may sufficiently understand the words of our Lord Jesus Christ now read out of the Gospel, let him bear with our ministry, whilst, if possible, with His assistance, we may, by treating thereof, cause that either all or many may understand that which a few are joyful of having understood for themselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I think that what the Lord says, “I am the light of the world, “is clear to those that have eyes, by which they are made partakers of this light: but they who have not eyes except in the flesh alone, wonder at what is said by the Lord Jesus Christ, “I am the light of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And perhaps there may not be wanting some one too who says with himself: Whether perhaps the Lord Christ is that sun which by its rising and setting causes the day?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there have not been wanting heretics who thought this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Manichæans have supposed that the Lord Christ is that sun which is visible to carnal eyes, exposed and public to be seen, not only by men, but by the beasts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the right faith of the Catholic Church rejects such a fiction, and perceives it to be a devilish doctrine: not only by believing acknowledges it to be such, but in the case of whom it can, proves it even by reasoning.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us therefore reject this kind of error, which the Holy Church has anathematized from the beginning.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us not suppose that the Lord Jesus Christ is this sun which we see rising from the east, setting in the west; to whose course succeeds night, whose rays are obscured by a cloud, which removes from place to place by a set motion: the Lord Christ is not such a thing as this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord Christ is not the sun that was made, but He by whom the sun was made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “all things were made by Him, and without Him was nothing made.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is therefore a Light which made this light of the sun: let us love this Light, let us long to understand it, let us thirst for the same; that, with itself for our guide, we may at length come to it, and that we may so live in it that we may never die.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is indeed that Light of which prophecy long ago going before thus sang in the psalm: “O Lord, Thou shalt save men and beasts; even as Thy mercy is multiplied, O God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These are the words of the holy psalm: mark ye what the ancient discourse of holy men of God did premise concerning such a light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Men,” saith it, “and beasts Thou shalt save, O Lord; even as Thy mercy is multiplied, O God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For since Thou art God, and hast manifold mercy, the same multiplicity of Thy mercy reaches not only to men whom Thou hast created in Thine own image, but even to the beasts which Thou hast made subservient to men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He who gives salvation to man, the same gives salvation also to the beast.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not blush to think this of the Lord thy God: nay, rather believe this and trust it, and see thou think not otherwise.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that saves thee, the same saves thy horse and thy sheep; to come to the very least, also thy hen: “Salvation is of the Lord,” and God saves these.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou art uneasy, thou questionest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I wonder why thou doubtest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Shall He disdain to save who deigned to create?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of the Lord is the saving of angels, of men, and of beasts: “Salvation is of the Lord.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just as no man is from himself, so no man is saved by himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore most truly and right well doth the psalm say, “O Lord, Thou shall save men and beasts.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Even as thy mercy is multiplied, O God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Thou art God, Thou hast created, Thou savest: Thou gavest being, Thou givest to be in health.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since, therefore, as the mercy of God is multiplied, men and beasts are saved by Him, have not men something else which God as Creator bestows on them, which He bestows not on the beasts?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is there no distinction between the living creature made after the image of God, and the living creature made subject to the image of God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Clearly there is: beyond that salvation common to us with the dumb animals, there is what God bestows on us, but not on them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Follow on in the same psalm: “But the sons of men shall hope under the covert of Thy wings.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Having now a salvation in common with their cattle, “the sons of men shall hope under the covert of Thy wings.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They have one salvation in fact, another in hope.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This salvation which is at present is common to men and cattle; but there is another which men hope for; and which they who hope for receive, they who despair of receive not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it saith, “The sons of men shall hope under covert of Thy wings.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And they that perseveringly hope are protected by Thee, lest they be cast down from their hope by the devil: “Under covert of Thy wings they shall hope.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If they shall hope, what shall they hope for, but for what the cattle shall not have?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “They shall be fully drunk with the fatness of Thy house; and from the torrent of Thy pleasure Thou shalt give them drink.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What sort of wine is that with which it is laudable to be drunk?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What sort of wine is that which disturbs not the mind, but directs it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What sort of wine is that which makes perpetually sane, and makes not insane by drinking?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “They shall be fully drunk.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “With the fatness of Thy house; and from the torrent of Thy pleasure Thou shalt give them drink.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because with Thee is the fountain of life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The very fountain of life walked on the earth, the same who said, “Whoso thirsts, let him come unto me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold the fountain!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But we begin to speak about the light, and to handle the question laid down from the Gospel concerning the light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we read how the Lord said, “I am the light of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thence arose a question, lest any one, carnally understanding this, should fancy this light to mean the sun: we came thence to the psalm, which having considered, we found meanwhile that the Lord is the fountain of life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Drink and live.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “With Thee,” it saith, “is the fountain of life;” therefore, “under the shadow of Thy wings the sons of men hope,” seeking to be full drunk with this fountain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But we were speaking of the Light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Follow on, then; for the prophet, having said, “With Thee is the fountain of life,” went on to add, “In Thy light shall we see light,”—God of God, Light of Light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By this Light the sun’s light was made; and the Light which made the sun, under which He also made us, was made under the sun for our sake.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That Light which made the sun, was made, I say, under the sun for our sake.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not despise the cloud of the flesh; with that cloud it is covered, not to be obscured, but to be moderated.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That unfailing Light, the Light of wisdom, speaking through the cloud of the flesh, says to men, “I am the light of the world; he that followeth me shall not walk in darkness, but shall have the light of life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How He has withdrawn thee from the eyes of the flesh, and recalled thee to the eyes of the heart!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is not enough to say, “Whoso followeth me shall not walk in darkness, but shall have light;” He added too, “of life;” even as it was there said, “For with Thee is the fountain of life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See thus, my brethren, how the words of the Lord agree with the truth of that psalm: both there, the light is put with the fountain of life, and by the Lord it is said, “light of life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But for bodily use, light and fountain are different things: our mouths seek a fountain, our eyes light; when we thirst we seek a fountain, when we are in darkness we seek light; and if we chance to thirst in the night, we kindle a light to come to a fountain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not so with God: light and fountain are the same thing: He who shines for thee that thou mayest see, the same flows for thee that thou mayest drink.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You see, then, my brethren, you see, if you see inwardly, what kind of light this is, of which the Lord says, “He that followeth me shall not walk in darkness.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Follow the sun, and let us see if thou wilt not walk in darkness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, by rising it comes forth to thee; it goes by its course towards the west.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps thy journey is towards the east: unless thou goest in a contrary direction to that in which it travels, thou wilt certainly err by following it, and instead of east wilt get to the west.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou follow it by land, thou wilt go wrong; if the mariner follow it by sea, he will go wrong.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Finally, it seems to thee, suppose, that thou must follow the sun, and thou also travellest thyself towards the west, whither it also travels; let us see after it has set if thou wilt not walk in darkness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See how, although thou art not willing to desert it, yet it will desert thee, to finish the day by necessity of its service.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But our Lord Jesus Christ, even when He was not manifest to all through the cloud of His flesh, was yet at the same time holding all things by the power of His wisdom.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thy God is whole everywhere: if thou fall not off from Him, He will never fall away from thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, “He that followeth me,” saith He, “shall not walk in darkness, but shall have the light of life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What He has promised, He put in a word of the future tense; for He says not has, but “shall have the light of life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet He does not say, He that shall follow me; but, he that does follow me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What it is our duty to do, He put in the present tense; but what He has promised to them that do it, He has indicated by a word of the future tense.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He that followeth, shall have.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That followeth now, shall have hereafter: followeth now by faith, shall have hereafter by sight.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, “whilst we are in the body,” saith the apostle, “we are absent from the Lord: for we walk by faith, not by sight.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When shall we walk by sight?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When we shall have the light of life, when we shall have come to that vision, when this night shall have passed away.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of that day, indeed, which is to arise, it is said, “In the morning I will stand near thee, and contemplate thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means “in the morning”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When the night of this world is over, when the terrors of temptations are over, when that lion which goeth about roaring in the night, seeking whom it may devour, is vanquished.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “In the morning I will stand near thee, and contemplate.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now what do we think, brethren, to be our duty for the present time, but what is again said in the psalm, “Every night through will I wash my couch; I will moisten my bed with my tears”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Every night through, saith he, I will weep; I will burn with desire for the light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord sees my desire: for another psalm says to Him, “All my desire is before Thee; and my groaning is not hid from Thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou desire gold?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou canst be seen; for, while seeking gold, thou wilt be manifest to men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou desire corn?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou askest one that has it; whom also thou informest, while seeking to get at that which thou desirest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou desire God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who sees, but God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From whom, then, dost thou seek God, as thou seekest bread, water, gold, silver, corn?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From whom dost thou seek God, except from God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is sought from Himself who has promised Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the soul extend her desire, and with more capacious bosom seek to comprehend that which “eye hath not seen, nor ear heard, nor hath entered into the heart of man.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Desire it we can, long for it we can, pant after it we can; but worthily conceive it, worthily unfold it in words, we cannot.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, my brethren, since the Lord says briefly, “I am the light of the world: he that followeth me shall not walk in darkness, but shall have the light of life;” in these words He has commanded one thing, promised another; let us do what He has commanded, that we may not with shameless face demand what He has promised; that He may not say to us in His judgment, Hast thou done what I commanded, that thou shouldest expect what I promised?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What hast Thou commanded, then, O Lord our God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He says to thee, That thou shouldest follow me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast sought counsel of life?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of what life, but of that of which it is said, “With Thee is the fountain of life”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A certain man heard it said to him,” Go, sell all that thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven; and come, follow me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He followed not, but went away sorrowful; he sought the “good Master,” went to Him as a teacher, and despised His teaching; he went away sorrowful, tied and bound by his lusts; he went away sorrowful, having a great load of avarice on his shoulders.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He toiled and fretted; and yet he thought that He, who was willing to rid him of his load, was not to be followed but forsaken.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But after the Lord has, by the gospel, cried aloud, “Come unto me, all ye that labor, and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest; take my yoke upon you, and learn of me, for I am meek and lowly in heart,” how many, on hearing the gospel, have done what that rich man, on hearing from His own mouth, did not do?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, let us do it now, let us follow the Lord; let us loose the fetters by which we are hindered from following Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And who is sufficient to loose such bonds, unless He help, to whom it is said, “Thou hast burst asunder my bonds”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of whom another psalm says, “The Lord looseth them that are in bonds; the Lord raiseth up them that are crushed and oppressed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what do they follow, who have been loosed and raised up, but the Light from which they hear, “I am the light of the world: he that followeth me shall not walk in darkness”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Lord gives light to the blind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore we, brethren, having the eye-salve of faith, are now enlightened.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For His spittle did before mingle with the earth, by which the eyes of him who was born blind were anointed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We, too, have been born blind of Adam, and have need of Him to enlighten us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He mixed spittle with clay: “The Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He mixed spittle with earth; hence it was predicted, “Truth has sprung from the earth;” and He said Himself, “I am the way, the truth, and the life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When we shall see face to face, we shall have the full fruition of the truth; for this also is promised to us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For who would dare hope for what God had not deigned either to promise or to give?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We shall see face to face.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The apostle says, “Now I know in part, now through a glass darkly; but then, face to face.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the Apostle John says in his epistle, “Beloved, now are we the sons of God; and it has not yet appeared what we shall be: we know that, when He shall appear, we shall be like Him; for we shall see Him even as He is.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is a great promise; if thou lovest, follow.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I do love, sayest thou, but by what way am I to follow?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the Lord thy God had said to thee, “I am the truth and the life,” in desiring truth and longing for life, thou mightest truly ask the way by which thou mightest come to these, and mightest say to thyself: A great thing is the truth, a great thing is the life, were there only the means whereby my soul might come thereto!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou ask by what way?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear Him say at the first, “I am the way.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Before He said whither, He premised by what way: “I am,” saith He, “the way.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The way whither?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And the truth and the life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: First, He told thee the way to come; then, whither to come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I am the way, I am the truth, I am the life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Remaining with the Father, the truth and life; putting on flesh, He became the way.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not said to thee, Labor in finding a way to come to the truth and life; this is not said to thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Sluggard, arise: the way itself has come to thee, and roused thee from thy sleep; if, however, it has roused thee, up and walk.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps thou art trying to walk, and art not able, because thy feet ache.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How come thy feet to ache?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Have they been running over rough places at the bidding of avarice?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the word of God has healed even the lame.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, thou sayest, I have my feet sound, but the way itself I see not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has also enlightened the blind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All this by faith, so long as we are absent from the Lord, dwelling in the body; but when we shall have traversed the way, and have reached the home itself, what shall be more joyful than we?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What shall be more blessed than we?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because nothing more at peace than we; for there will be no rebelling against a man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now, brethren, it is difficult for us to be without strife.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have indeed been called to concord, we are commanded to have peace among ourselves; to this we must give our endeavor, and strain with all our might, that we may come at last to the most perfect peace; but at present we are at strife, very often with those whose good we are seeking.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is one who goes astray, thou wishest to lead him to the way; he resists, thou strivest with him: the pagan resists thee, thou disputest against the errors of idols and devils; a heretic resists, thou disputest against other doctrines of devils; a bad catholic is not willing to live aright, thou rebukest even thy brother within; he dwells with thee in the house, and seeks the paths of ruin; thou art inflamed with eager passion to put him right, that thou mayest render to the Lord a good account of both concerning him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How many necessities of strife there are on every side!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Very often one is overcome with weariness, and says to himself, “What have I to do with bearing with gainsayers, bearing with those who render evil for good?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I wish to benefit them, they are willing to perish; I wear out my life in strife; I have no peace; besides, I make enemies of those whom I ought to have as friends, if they regarded the good will of him that seeks their good: what business is it of mine to endure this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let me return to myself, I will be kept to myself, I will call upon my God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do return to thyself, thou findest strife there.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou hast begun to follow God, thou findest strife there.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What strife, sayest thou, do I find?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The flesh lusteth against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold thou art thyself, thou art alone, thou art with thyself; behold, thou art bearing with no other person, but yet thou seest another law in thy members warring against the law of thy mind, and taking thee captive in the law of sin, which is in thy members.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Cry aloud, then, and cry to God, that He may give thee peace from the inner strife: “O wretched man that I am, who shall deliver me from the body of this death?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The grace of God through our Lord Jesus Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because, “He that followeth me,” saith He, “shall not walk in darkness, but shall have the light of life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All strife ended, immortality shall follow; for “the last enemy, death, shall be destroyed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what peace will this be?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “This corruptible must put on incorruption, and this mortal must put on immortality.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To which that we may come (for it will then be in reality), let us now follow in hope Him who said, “I am the light of the world: he that followeth me shall not walk in darkness, but shall have the light of life.”
